summary: Clear, well‑balanced audience recording captures a mixed‑energy 1995 Deer Creek show with standout renditions of Dire Wolf, Here Comes Sunshine and other classics.
review: The tape is praised for its crisp, front‑of‑house sound – drums are crystal clear and crowd noise minimal – making it one of the better recordings from the turbulent July 2 1995 Deer Creek concert.  Musically the show receives mixed feedback: some fans note a lack of energy and Jerry’s fading voice, yet many classic numbers (Dire Wolf, Here Comes Sunshine, Desolation Row, etc.) are described as sounding great even 30 years later.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently praised (excellent), while show performance is described as both mediocre and containing several standout songs, leading to an overall good‑to‑great rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear front‑of‑house mix; drums crisp, vocals solid, minimal crowd bleed. Reviewers note mics close to speaker stacks and a well‑balanced mono monitor mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert suffered from a chaotic atmosphere and a perceived dip in energy, but the setlist still delivered many beloved classics that sound surprisingly fresh on this recording.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice described as strong yet fading; flashes of greatness noted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Solid guitar work and flawless vocal delivery on his songs.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums are exceptionally clear and well‑mixed; Rhythm Devils keep a steady groove.
